description: Evaluates how enterprise security governance is established, operated, measured, and enforced across the organization—decision rights, oversight, governance metrics, responsibility and leadership, segmentation strategy governance, external expert input, and AI-era governance aligned to DORA and the EU AI Act.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: The enterprise security governance function is established, funded, and operating effectively with clear scope and outcomes reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Documented governance charter and scope
Operating model, stakeholders, and delivery cadence
Continuous improvement and stakeholder feedback

capabilities.name: Governance metrics
capabilities.description: Governance defines measurable outcomes and uses KPIs/KRIs—including policy exception rate, decision cycle time, and board reporting completeness—to drive decisions and communicate risk to leadership at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Defined KPIs/KRIs tied to governance outcomes
Leadership and board reporting cadence
Evidence that metrics inform decisions and prioritization
DORA and regulatory governance reporting metrics where applicable

capabilities.name: Governance responsibility model and leadership
capabilities.description: Roles, committees, decision rights, and a named governance leader operate with documented RACI, escalation paths, and accountability for security governance outcomes.
evaluation_focus: Defined governance bodies, responsibilities, and decision scope
Documented RACI for key security decisions
Clear escalation paths for high-risk decisions and exceptions
Named leader with clear mandate and delegated authority
Engagement model with executives and delivery stakeholders
Accountability for governance decisions and outcomes

capabilities.name: Segmentation governance strategy
capabilities.description: Governance defines and reviews user, workload (micro-segmentation), and network (macro-segmentation) strategies—including criteria, enforcement principles, exceptions, and effectiveness measurement—at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Defined user segmentation criteria and governed identity categories
Defined micro-segmentation principles and workload scope
Defined network segmentation zones and allowable traffic patterns
Enterprise-wide formalization and implementation of segmentation strategies
Exception governance and measurement of segmentation effectiveness
Quarterly review of segmentation governance strategies

capabilities.name: External expert reference
capabilities.description: Governance incorporates external expert input or benchmarks—advisory, threat intelligence, peer benchmarks, audit/assurance, and AI-era threat research—to improve decisions and validate direction.
evaluation_focus: Use of independent expertise or benchmarks to inform governance
Evidence of periodic external review or validation
Clear mechanism to incorporate findings into decisions and improvements

capabilities.name: AI-era security governance
capabilities.description: Governance bodies account for AI adoption, dual-use model risk, EU AI Act obligations, and DORA ICT risk management in decision rights, oversight, and investment prioritization.
evaluation_focus: Governance charter addresses AI system oversight and accountability
EU AI Act and sector AI compliance obligations reflected in governance scope
DORA ICT risk governance integrated into security governance cadence

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
